title: papaver henrici
intro: Papaver henrici, also known as Meconella henrici, is a rare, annual plant species native to the mountainous regions of the southwestern United States and Mexico. It is a member of the poppy family and is prized for its medicinal properties, particularly its alkaloids, which have been shown to have analgesic, anti-inflammatory, and antispasmodic effects. The plant is used in herbal preparations to alleviate various health issues, including pain, insomnia, and respiratory problems, and its extracts have been traditionally used to treat coughs, colds, and other respiratory ailments. Herbal preparations of Papaver henrici can be made into teas, tinctures, or infused oils, and are often used in folk medicine to promote relaxation and reduce stress.
